title: quisqualis latialata
intro:
Quisqualis latialata, also known as Rangoon creeper, is a flowering plant native to tropical Asia. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects, which can aid in treating various health conditions. The plant has been reported to have health benefits such as reducing fever, relieving pain, and treating respiratory issues. Herbal preparations of Quisqualis latialata include tea infusions and decoctions, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.

Note: However, there is no reliable information available on Quisqualis latialata plant and its medicinal properties.
